title: Le Monde's point-by-point breakdown of the fragile US-Iran framework agreement
subhed: After more than 100 days of war, the United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending their conflict. The document will be followed by negotiations to address issues it does not resolve. Le Monde publishes the full text, along with context and analysis.

title: Russia's shadow looms behind several sabotage and espionage attempts on French soil
Israel and Hezbollah agree to ceasefire after deadly exchanges between the two sides
After US signals another review of its military presence in Europe, allies grapple with Washington's ambiguities
subhed: On June 18, the US defense secretary announced a 'review' of the distribution of American NATO forces on the continent. The decision has raised concerns, even though it is being presented as a simple 'adjustment,' a senior US defense official told Le Monde.

title: In Poland, Jordan Bardella dreams of leading an alliance between Europe's right-wing and far-right parties
subhed: During a visit to Warsaw and the Belarusian border on June 18 and 19, the president of the Rassemblement National accelerated efforts to build an alliance with European conservatives and liberals – going so far as to distance himself from the Eurosceptic and sovereigntist stance Marine Le Pen has promoted for two decades.

title: USA down Australia 2-0 to reach World Cup knockout rounds
subhed: The Americans have made a dream start to the tournament with Friday's victory following a 4-1 demolition of Paraguay in their opening game last week. Co-host Mexico has also sealed its place in the knockout rounds after beating South Korea 1-0 on Thursday.

title: Fête de la Musique: Our picks for concerts not to miss
subhed: Le Monde's Culture desk offers its selection of concerts taking place on Sunday, June 21, as part of the national Fête de la Musique, which has become a global phenomenon.

Ancient fault discovery means Paris region may face higher seismic risk
'They call us misfits, degenerates': Free parties fall under French government scrutiny
subhed: Thousands of people gathered on June 6 in the south of France for a rave party, despite the events being banned locally until the end of the year. Participants defend them as among the last spaces of freedom outside of commercial and institutional frameworks.

title: In Trump's polarized America, empathy faces unanimous opposition
subhed: Once valued in American public life, empathy now faces criticism from the far right, which sees it as a sign of weakness. The left, too, is questioning its relevance: In a world where brutality prevails, can we still afford to show understanding?

title: French security replaces Palantir with domestic firm ChapsVision, a complicated choice
subhed: Used since the 2015 terrorist attacks, the controversial American company's data analysis software is set to be gradually replaced by that of a French provider. The long-awaited decision presents a significant technological challenge.
